items.title: AI Now #1 Cause of Job Cuts — Third Consecutive Month, Record 40% of May Total
items.idea: Challenger May job cuts rose +16% m/m to 97.0k — the highest May total since 2020. AI led all reasons for cuts for the THIRD consecutive month at 38.6k, fully 40% of all May cuts, a record high since Challenger began tracking AI as a reason in 2023. Technology led all sectors at 38.2k — highest monthly total since August 2024; YTD tech cuts of 123.7k are up 66% y/y. Economic conditions-related reasons accounted for 53% of the month's total. Hiring plans rose to 19.5k with Tech leading at 11.3k; YTD hiring of 80.5k has now narrowly topped 2025's pace for the first time all year.
items.angle: Structural AI displacement is accelerating in tech, but the hiring data carries a signal: firms are simultaneously cutting and hiring — hollowing out mid-level work, not eliminating the sector. The 66% YTD increase in tech cuts while hiring rises = bifurcation, not extinction.
items.tweetA: AI just became the #1 reason for job cuts for the third month in a row. 40% of all May layoffs — a record high since Challenger started tracking it in 2023. Tech firms are cutting their highest headcount since August 2024. This is not a trend. It is a structural shift.
items.tweetB: Buried in the Challenger data: tech cuts are up 66% y/y (123.7k YTD) but tech hiring is also picking up (11.3k in May alone). The jobs being eliminated and the jobs being created are not the same jobs. Welcome to the bifurcation economy.

charts.desc: May cuts by sector and by reason; YTD comparisons vs 2024 and 2025.

items.title: Dollar About to Accelerate to 103-104; Gold Below 3,800; 10-Year Still Going to 5%
items.idea: Dale Pinkert argues the dollar index did not follow through to the downside and is now challenging 99.5 — he expects an acceleration phase toward 103-104. If the dollar accelerates upward, he targets gold below 3,800 and silver at $50 or lower (current support at 70). He believes 10-year yields are still going to 5% long-term; if Friday payrolls disappoint, he would buy the 10-year at 4.30 as a tactical trade. S&P pullback target: 7,100. VIX at ~16; he would not be short vol if VIX reclaims 18.
items.angle: This is the unifying framework for today's moves. If the dollar accelerates, the reflexive weak-dollar buy-gold trade unwinds, bonds come under pressure, and rate-sensitive assets get hit again. Watch DXY 99.5 as the trigger level.
items.tweetA: The dollar did not follow through to the downside. Now challenging 99.5 — Dale Pinkert is targeting 103-104 in the acceleration phase. If he is right: gold below 3,800, silver at $50, 10yr yield to 5%. That changes the setup for almost everything. $DXY $GLD $TLT
items.tweetB: 'If you get the dollar right, you get everything right.' Dollar index at 99.5 and accelerating. Gold at the top of its range. Silver support at 70. This is the trade that nobody is talking about while everyone watches AVGO.

charts.desc: DXY challenging 99.5 with 103-104 target; gold downside levels; silver support at 70; 10yr yield with 5% target. CHART WORTH SAVING — DXY setup.

items.title: Nat Gas $6-$10 by Year-End; Long Side of Food Will Be Best Long Entry Next Year
items.idea: Dale Pinkert is calling for nat gas to reach $6-$10 by end of 2026 or early 2027, noting the market has legs and is bullish unless it takes out $2.82. He made this call while taking profits on a prior position at $6. On agriculture: Long side of food will be one of the best long entries that pays off big next year. Cocoa: continue buying dips. Buying opportunity in wheat and corn around the Summer Solstice. Soybeans holding up but look vulnerable. BOJ must raise rates at next meeting; expects yen intervention around 160/USD.
items.angle: Nat gas at $6-10 is a large structural call — data center power demand plus LNG export buildout plus potential cold winter. The agricultural call dovetails with La Nina tail risk and supply disruptions. These are the slow-moving trades that compound over 12 months.
items.tweetA: One trader with a strong track record just called for nat gas reaching $6-$10 by year-end. 'This market has legs.' Data center power demand, LNG export buildout, and winter risk are all tailwinds. Sub-$4 nat gas feels cheap if even one of those factors surprises to the upside. $UNG
items.tweetB: 'Long side of food will be one of the best long entries that pays off big next year.' The veteran trader who called the nat gas move is now looking at wheat, corn, and cocoa. Climate disruption plus Ukraine supply noise plus central banks cutting to avoid recession = food inflation is not done.

charts.desc: Nat gas chart with profitable trade exit and upside targets; agricultural commodity charts.

items.title: AVGO Down 15% — $2T Company Triggers Semi Cascade; SPY Gaps Below 8 EMA Second Time Since April
items.idea: Broadcom's earnings triggered a cascade across the semiconductor complex. AVGO, a $2 trillion company and one of the 10 largest in the world, fell 15% intraday. SPY gapped below the 8 EMA for the second time since the April ceasefire. Beneficiaries: Dow +1.63%, Russell +1.29%. Bulltard says the three things that could derail this bull market are: oil prices ramping, inflation ramping, or a hiccup in the AI trade — and today delivered the third. Next key SPY support: 21 EMA at 743.31.
items.angle: AVGO's ER could be an inflection point for the AI infrastructure narrative. If Broadcom's results signal that hyperscaler capex is plateauing or that AI chip demand is lumpier than expected, the entire AI complex is at risk. Watch whether the rotation to Dow/Russell holds.
items.tweetA: AVGO just fell 15%. One of the 10 largest companies in the world. SPY gapped below the 8 EMA for the second time since April. The three market-killers: oil ramping, inflation, or an AI hiccup. Today: third scenario activated. Watch 743 on $SPY — that is the line.
items.tweetB: $2 trillion company down 15%. And the market is UP. Dow +1.6%, Russell +1.3%. Capital is rotating — out of AI infrastructure, into cyclicals and small caps. The bull is not dead. It is changing shape.

charts.desc: SPY below 8 EMA for the second time since April ceasefire; AVGO intraday selloff chart. CHART WORTH SAVING.

items.title: Trump's Revised AI Executive Order Is Largely Toothless — Three Failure Nodes
items.idea: After aborting a signing on May 21 (Trump: I don't want to do anything to get in the way of our lead), the White House posted Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security on June 2. The EO creates a voluntary program for AI companies to give the federal government 30-day advance access to frontier models — but does not define covered frontier model, has no enforcement mechanism, no funding, and removed the original vetting language under industry pressure. Three failure nodes: (1) voluntary = unenforceable; (2) covered frontier model undefined = lawyers will exploit; (3) original vetting framework replaced by collaboration. Anthropic already refused to share Mythos with DoD.
items.angle: This is the regulatory arbitrage trade: US AI companies face essentially zero government-mandated safety requirements while the EU AI Act is binding. This preserves the speed advantage for American AI labs at the cost of guardrails — a tail risk that could crystallize suddenly.
items.tweetA: Trump's AI executive order: the government gets access to frontier models 30 days before release. Except it is voluntary. Frontier model is not defined. There is no testing requirement. No funding. Silicon Valley rewrote this in 15 days — the original vetting language is gone. This is not governance.
items.tweetB: The original Trump AI EO included a framework to vet national security risks of frontier models. The signed version? Voluntary collaboration where AI companies can choose not to participate. Anthropic already refused to share Mythos with DoD. The precedent for opting out is already set.

items.title: First Senate Candidate to Run on AI Safety: Token Tax, Human-in-the-Loop Mandates, Chip Export Controls
items.idea: Michigan Senate candidate Mallory McMorrow unveiled the most detailed AI policy agenda in any 2026 US Senate race: an AI Workforce Reinvestment Fund requiring companies that automate jobs to fund retraining; a token tax on AI usage to bolster state aid programs; human-in-the-loop mandates for healthcare, hiring, and military; stronger chip export controls; third-party reviews of frontier models before deployment. Endorsed by Elizabeth Warren. a16z's Leading the Future super PAC already spent $4M+ against AI safety candidate Alex Bores in New York. OpenAI is building a 1GW data center in Michigan.
items.angle: The token tax concept is novel — a use-fee on AI compute that could become a template for other states. If McMorrow wins the Michigan primary, these ideas enter the Senate mainstream. The a16z anti-safety PAC spending is the real canary: industry is treating this as existential.
items.tweetA: First Senate candidate with a serious AI agenda: token tax on AI compute, human-in-the-loop for healthcare and military, third-party model reviews before deployment. Elizabeth Warren endorsed her. A16Z's super PAC already spent $4M against similar candidates in New York. The regulatory clock just started.
items.tweetB: Michigan is quietly the most important AI policy battleground in 2026. OpenAI is building a 1GW data center there. A construction worker told McMorrow: I want to build the thing but I also want to make sure it does not surveil me or kill kids in another country. That tension is going to define the next Congress.

items.title: $6B Sell-Side Research Industry Opens to AI Workflows — Aiera, IBKR+Claude, Robinhood MCP Server
items.idea: Aiera (backed by BofA, Citi, Deutsche Bank, Evercore, HSBC) launched a platform allowing sell-side research to enter AI workflows through secure API and MCP connectors with entitlement checks. The sell-side research market is an estimated $6 billion annually — $500M/year at Citi alone. Key bottleneck: 40% of all sell-side research is earnings previews that analysts produce before client calls that clients often don't value. Parallel developments: Interactive Brokers added Anthropic's Claude; Robinhood will allow third-party AI agents through its MCP server; retail trading hit a record 35% of total market activity in April 2025.
items.angle: $6B in sell-side research is about to be consumed by AI agents. The analyst writing what a model can generate is 18 months from being automated out. Value shifts to proprietary channel checks, original thesis, and relationships.
items.tweetA: Sell-side research is a $6B/year industry. 40% of it is routine earnings previews that nobody reads. Aiera just opened a secure API for AI agents to access this content with entitlement checks. The analyst who writes what a model can generate is 18 months from being automated out.
items.tweetB: Interactive Brokers added Anthropic's Claude. Robinhood is opening an MCP server for third-party AI trading agents. Retail trading already hit 35% of all market activity in April 2025 — a record. What happens to price discovery when every retail investor has an AI hedge fund manager?

items.title: a16z Hires Former Biden NSC Cybersecurity Chief as GP — Mission: Help America and Her Allies
items.idea: a16z announced Anne Neuberger (former Deputy National Security Advisor for Cyber and Emerging Technology under Biden) as their newest General Partner and Head of Global Affairs. The mandate: drive tech partnerships with allied nations on AI, robotics, defense modernization, cybersecurity, and supply chain resilience. Renamed Investor Relations team to Global Partnerships, now targeting sovereign wealth funds as LPs. Tokyo office opened. 100+ international investments to date.
items.angle: a16z has completed its transformation from venture firm to geopolitical actor. Hiring the NSC's cyber chief signals deep embedding in the defense-industrial complex — positioning for defense tech procurement, allied nation AI deals, and sovereign LP relationships.
items.tweetA: a16z just hired the Biden White House's top cybersecurity official as a General Partner. Their stated mission: help not just founders, but America and her allies. They renamed IR to Global Partnerships and are courting sovereign wealth funds. Venture capital as an instrument of geopolitical strategy.
items.tweetB: The old a16z: find the next social app. The new a16z: build bridges between US tech and allied nations on AI, robotics, defense, and supply chains. Anne Neuberger ran cybersecurity for the entire US government. Now she runs global affairs for the most powerful VC on earth.

items.source: The Dark Side of the Boom
items.title: SpaceX IPO: $1.8T Valuation, $75B Raise — Largest in Market History; JPMorgan Mobilizes 90 Locations
items.idea: SpaceX is targeting approximately $1.8 trillion valuation and $75 billion raise — the largest IPO in market history. Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley lead the syndicate. JPMorgan rolled out a nationwide event featuring CEO Jamie Dimon, Mary Callahan Erdoes, and SpaceX executives for 2,500 wealthy private clients across 90 locations. The deal is moving beyond institutional buyers into the wealth management channel. If SpaceX trades well, it opens the IPO window for OpenAI and Anthropic.
items.angle: This is a referendum on whether public markets can absorb $75B at a valuation requiring near-perfect execution across multiple high-risk industries simultaneously. History says mega-IPOs at peak excitement underperform. The real signal is what happens to OpenAI and Anthropic valuations the week after.
items.tweetA: $75B raise. $1.8 trillion valuation. Jamie Dimon doing a roadshow at 90 JPMorgan branches for 2,500 private wealth clients. This is the largest IPO in market history. If it trades well, OpenAI and Anthropic are next. The 2026 IPO window is opening with a $75 billion key.
items.tweetB: Every major tech IPO in recent history arrived at peak excitement with peak expectations and delivered mixed first-year results. Morningstar says investors may find better entry points post-IPO. Exceptional companies do not always make exceptional investments at IPO prices. Apply that to $1.8 trillion.

items.source: Dwarkesh Podcast / Alex Imas & Phil Trammell
items.title: 'One Robot Turns Into Many Robots Next Year, But the Number of Ballerinas Is the Same' — What Remains Scarce After AGI
items.idea: Economists Alex Imas and Phil Trammell argue AGI does not eliminate scarcity — it redirects it. AI labor can compound exponentially but truly scarce inputs (human attention, authentic experience, physical presence, natural resources) become more valuable, not less. Three unanswered questions: How do you optimally tax and redistribute AI-generated wealth? How do nations not in the AI supply chain index into the gains? Is there any world where inequality does not explode?
items.angle: This is the investment framework for the next decade: what cannot be replicated? Authentic human experience (live events, handmade goods, in-person services), physical scarcity (land, energy, water), and genuine creative novelty. These are not just cultural observations — they are asset classes in an AGI world.
items.tweetA: 'One robot turns into many robots next year, but the number of ballerinas is the same.' AGI does not end scarcity — it redirects it. Things that cannot be copied become worth more. Authentic presence. Physical space. Genuine creativity. Energy. Those are the asset classes for the next decade.
items.tweetB: Three questions nobody on Wall Street is asking: How do countries NOT in the AI supply chain capture any of the gains? What is the optimal redistribution model for AI-generated wealth? Is inequality explosion the default outcome? Two economists, 76 minutes, no easy answers.

charts.desc: Charts inside the video — worth watching.

items.title: BTC 4-Year Cycle: September/October 2026 Bottom Expected, $50K Trough — Start DCA Below $65K
items.idea: The DeFi Investor maps 2026 BTC price action against midterm years 2014, 2018, 2022 — all bottomed approximately one year after the post-halving peak. Cycle bottom expected September/October 2026. Action plan: DCA daily below $65K; expected bottom ~$50K; worst case $40K only if a major crypto company implodes FTX-style. Key lesson: people who bought BTC at $16k-$20k in 2022 and held until new ATH outperformed more than 95% of altcoin traders.
items.angle: The 4-year cycle is contested academically but remarkably consistent empirically. If the bottom is Sep/Oct 2026, we are in the accumulation window. People who waited for $14K in 2022 missed the entire rally.
items.tweetA: 2014: BTC bottomed ~1 year after the post-halving peak. 2018: same. 2022: same. 2026: following the same chart exactly. Expected bottom: September/October. Expected trough: $50K. People who bought at $16K in 2022 and held beat 95% of altcoin traders. Simple strategy still wins.
items.tweetB: Everyone is waiting for $40K BTC. The DeFi Investor says: that only happens if a major crypto company implodes like FTX in 2022. Base case is $50K in Q3. If you wait for maximum pain, you might be waiting past the bottom — like those who waited for $14K that never came.

charts.desc: BTC ROI chart overlaying midterm years 2014, 2018, 2022, 2026. CHART WORTH SAVING.

items.title: BTC Hits Weekly 200 EMA + Fair Value Zone at $53K; $80M in Shorts; Saylor Down $10B
items.idea: BTC hit its weekly 200 EMA and is closing in on the long-term fair value zone at approximately $53K. Ultra-massive shorts ($80M+) have appeared at current levels. The altcoin season index is almost flashing green. Caution on STRC-backed stable products given Saylor drama. Saylor is currently down $10 billion on Strategy's BTC position. HYPE flipped SOL in price today. Active loans on Aave V4 doubled in the past 30 days. Crypto protocols generated $65M in fees in the last 24 hours.
items.angle: Weekly 200 EMA has historically been major long-term support with massive short squeezes. $80M shorts + fair value zone + altseason index near green = setup that often precedes violent reversals. Saylor forced-liquidation scenario is the tail risk that changes everything.
items.tweetA: BTC just hit the weekly 200 EMA. Long-term fair value zone: $53K. $80 million in shorts sitting right here. Saylor is down $10 billion. This is either the best long-term entry since $20K — or the start of a forced-liquidation cascade. The difference is whether Strategy gets margin-called.
items.tweetB: HYPE flipped SOL in price today. Active loans on Aave V4 doubled in 30 days. Crypto protocols: $65M in fees in the last 24 hours. The protocols are printing money. The tokens are down. At some point reality and price converge.

charts.desc: BTC weekly 200 EMA; long-term fair value zone at $53K; 5-year crypto protocol revenue chart. CHART WORTH SAVING — fair value zone.

items.title: STRC Explained: The Bitcoin-Backed Instrument That Converted a Skeptical CFO — 'You Cannot Print Energy'
items.idea: Prevalon Energy CFO Ben Hunnewell — who builds power plants and battery storage — became the first CFO to hold STRC, Strategy's Bitcoin-backed yield instrument. He started as a Bitcoin skeptic, then a Bitcoin holder who still did not think it belonged on a corporate balance sheet, until STRC solved the volatility problem for operating companies. Key insight: 'You cannot print energy, data centers, or the things that matter most.' He argues some AI energy headlines are good propaganda and the 23 Hiroshima bombs data center power claim is misleading.
items.angle: STRC represents a new corporate adoption vector for Bitcoin — not as a speculative bet but as a yield-bearing instrument that sidesteps price volatility. Tom Lee's Bitmine is already copying the structure for ETH. If it proliferates, it dramatically expands corporate BTC treasury exposure.
items.tweetA: A CFO who builds power plants used to make fun of Bitcoin investors. Then he became the first CFO ever to hold STRC. His reason: operating companies cannot ride BTC volatility — they need the cash for payroll. STRC solved this. The institutional Bitcoin adoption story just got a fundamentally different chapter.
items.tweetB: 'You cannot print energy, data centers, or the things that matter most.' Prevalon Energy's CFO on the AI energy crisis: the scary headlines are mostly propaganda. The 23 Hiroshima bombs data center claim is not what it sounds like. The physical constraints on AI buildout are different from the media narrative.

items.title: DeFi Weekly: CFTC Legalizes Perpetuals; Grayscale HYPE ETF; OpenSea Perps; Radiant Shuts Down
items.idea: Five material DeFi developments: (1) CFTC legalized on-chain perpetuals in the US — watershed regulatory event for DeFi; (2) Grayscale launched Hyperliquid Staking ETF; (3) OpenSea announced perps DEX powered by Hyperliquid; (4) Radiant Capital announced shutdown; (5) Cap ICO starting June 10. Also: Sky+Pendle fixed-rate sUSDS product; Morpho Midnight whitepaper (fixed-rate, fixed-term credit markets); NEAR Intents surpassed $20B all-time cross-chain volume; Zodial launched as first portfolio-margin lending protocol on Solana.
items.angle: The CFTC legalizing perps is the single biggest regulatory development in DeFi this cycle. US retail investors can now legally access on-chain perpetuals — the most popular DeFi product globally. Hyperliquid just received legal access to the largest retail trading market on earth.
items.tweetA: The CFTC just legalized on-chain perpetuals in the US. This is bigger than any individual token move. Hyperliquid, GMX, and dYdX just got handed legal access to 330 million potential US retail users. HYPE flipped SOL in price today. Timing.
items.tweetB: Radiant Capital is shutting down. Morpho is building fixed-rate, fixed-term credit markets from scratch. Cap ICO June 10. OpenSea launching a perps DEX. The DeFi credit and derivatives layer is being rebuilt after the exploits. Protocols that survived and rebuilt win the cycle.

items.title: Hormuz Transit Traffic Falls; China Counterbalancing Middle Eastern Oil Loss; Iran Nuclear Threat Rising
items.idea: Commodity Context's June 2026 Context Call previews updated Strait of Hormuz transit data. Key finding: China is counterbalancing the loss of Middle Eastern exports. Oil is back above $100 as Iran supply risks continue. TMAD Commodities flagged an Iran Nuclear Threat increase, positioning in $GLD $USO $SLV $URA — the uranium ticker is notable as a nuclear escalation hedge.
items.angle: China counterbalancing Hormuz disruption through alternative supply channels insulates Beijing while deepening bilateral energy relationships with producers bypassing Western sanctions. $URA inclusion in an Iran hedge signals markets may begin pricing a nuclear risk premium.
items.tweetA: Oil is back above $100. Hormuz transit data shows China is actively counterbalancing the loss of Middle Eastern exports — locking in alternative energy relationships as geopolitical risk rises. This is not just a supply shock story. It is a China strategic positioning story.
items.tweetB: TMAD is positioning in $GLD $USO $SLV $URA as Iran nuclear threat increases. The $URA inclusion is the tell — when a commodity trader includes uranium in an Iran escalation hedge, they are not just trading oil. They are trading a nuclear risk premium.

charts.desc: Hormuz transit traffic chart from June 2026 context call.

items.title: Mantle Q1 2026: Aave V3 from $0 to $547M TVL in 7 Weeks; RWA +27%; Token Down 27%
items.idea: Mantle's Q1 2026 shows institutional products scaling fast while retail cooled sharply. Aave V3 launched Feb 11 and scaled from zero to $547.1M TVL — one of the fastest protocol growth curves in DeFi history. RWA TVL grew 27.4% QoQ to $247.5M (Maple's syrupUSDT + Bybit's xStocks). DeFi TVL all-time high of $648M. Network assembled full AI agent stack: ERC-8004 identity, AI Agent Skills, Agent Scaffold, x402 payments. $2.4B Mantle Treasury diversifying. But: MNT token fell 27.6%, daily active addresses dropped 54.5% to network low. However 87% of activity from returning users; transactions/address rose 8.4 to 14.7.
items.angle: Mantle has a $2.4B treasury, full AI agent infrastructure, Bybit integration, and Aave V3 scaling faster than almost any protocol ever — and the token is down 27%. The divergence between institutional usage and retail activity is striking.
items.tweetA: Aave V3 launched on Mantle on Feb 11. By March 31: $547M TVL. Zero to half a billion in 7 weeks — one of the fastest protocol growth curves in DeFi history. MNT is down 27.6%. The network is working. The market has not noticed yet.
items.tweetB: Mantle has a $2.4B treasury, a full AI agent stack (ERC-8004 + x402 payments), Bybit integration, and a DeFi TVL all-time high. Daily active addresses hit a network low. Either institutions are scaling it while retail does not know — or there is a structural engagement problem. Q2 will answer this.

charts.desc: Charts in full Messari report — worth opening.

items.title: Stanford: AI Legal Tutors Beat Law Professors 75% of the Time; Meta Halts Employee Tracking; Suno $400M at $5.4B
items.idea: Four material AI data points: (1) Stanford study shows AI legal tutors outperform law professors in 75% of assessments — direct challenge to legal education ROI and law firm staffing; (2) Suno raised $400M+ at $5.4B valuation as first music AI company with industry licensing; (3) Microsoft + Mayo Clinic building frontier healthcare AI on anonymized patient data; (4) Meta forced to halt employee keystroke and mouse-movement tracking after a 1,500-worker petition; (5) Gemini Android agent hijacked via WhatsApp prompt injection exploit.
items.angle: The Stanford law study is an extraordinary quantitative finding: if AI outperforms law professors 75% of the time, the ROI on legal education just collapsed. The Gemini WhatsApp hijack is the most underreported security story — any WhatsApp message can now potentially compromise an AI agent connected to your phone.
items.tweetA: Stanford study: AI legal tutors beat law professors 75% of the time. Law school: $60K/year. Law professors: $250K salary. Lawyers: $500/hour. This is the clearest quantitative signal that professional services are in the crosshairs of AI displacement. Not in five years. Right now.
items.tweetB: Gemini Android agent was hijacked via a single WhatsApp message — a prompt injection that took control of the AI assistant. This is the most dangerous AI security vector nobody is discussing: once your phone AI is connected to everything, a single malicious message can compromise your entire digital life.

items.title: DuckDuckGo Reports Largest Search Traffic Ever as Google's AI Push Alienates Users; Burry Shorts Palantir
items.idea: DuckDuckGo reported its largest search traffic in history as Google's AI search feature drives users toward privacy-preserving alternatives. Goldman Sachs spotted an Nvidia-linked market shift not seen in 13 years. Michael Burry dropped a rare technical warning on Palantir, reiterating his short position based on a head-and-shoulders pattern. Jim Cramer urged caution on Marvell. Blue Origin's rocket explosion has made SpaceX even harder to catch. A market Nvidia once dominated is slipping away fast.
items.angle: Google's AI search may be the most consequential self-inflicted wound in the history of tech moats. If DuckDuckGo is hitting records, the world's most dominant internet monopoly is actively training users to seek alternatives. The long-term implications for GOOGL ad revenue are not priced in.
items.tweetA: DuckDuckGo just reported its largest search traffic ever — because Google's AI is alienating users. The world's most dominant internet monopoly is actively driving users to alternatives by forcing AI into search. The moat that took 25 years to build is showing its first real cracks.
items.tweetB: Michael Burry is short Palantir, citing a head-and-shoulders pattern. Blue Origin's rocket explosion makes SpaceX harder to catch. Goldman spots a 13-year Nvidia market shift. Big cap tech today: an active minefield where the old winners may not be the next winners.

items.title: Citadel — $90B Generated Since 1990 — Is Paying Other Hedge Funds for Trading Ideas; AlphaSense at $7.5B
items.idea: Ken Griffin's Citadel, the most profitable hedge fund since inception ($90.4B after fees since 1990), is preparing to launch a program collecting trading insights from other hedge funds in exchange for fees to feed its quantitative strategies. This validates Numerai's crowdsourced alpha model — JPMorgan committed $500M to Numerai. AlphaSense raised at $7.5B valuation. Perplexity reached $500M revenue with a real-time local-cloud inference routing system splitting compute between device and cloud.
items.angle: If Citadel cannot generate enough alpha internally, it confirms that markets have structurally become harder to trade. The only sustainable edge is more data, more signal sources. This is the moat that AI can monetize — not just trading AI but signal aggregation at scale.
items.tweetA: The most profitable hedge fund in history — $90 billion returned since 1990 — is now paying other hedge funds for trading ideas. If Citadel cannot generate enough signal internally, nobody can. Markets are harder to alpha than ever before. The only edge left is more data.
items.tweetB: Perplexity hit $500M revenue. AlphaSense raised at $7.5B. Citadel is buying trading signals from other hedge funds. The AI-finance infrastructure layer is being priced at multiples that assume this wave is structural and permanent. That may be exactly right.

items.title: Historical Data: Big Tech IPOs Have Mixed First-Year Records — Warning as SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic Loom
items.idea: Data from Truist's Keith Lerner and Trace Cohen's ValueAddVC.com shows large tech IPOs generally deliver mixed results in their first year. Exceptional companies often disappoint public market investors at launch when IPO pricing already reflects perfection. This arrives exactly as SpaceX ($1.8T target), OpenAI, and Anthropic are approaching public markets. Morningstar separately warns investors may find more attractive entry points after the SpaceX IPO.
items.angle: The historical IPO data is a check on the narrative that Day 1 allocation is an automatic win. If SpaceX opens at $1.8T and underperforms over the next year, the downstream effect on OpenAI and Anthropic valuation expectations could be significant.
items.tweetA: Historical data from Truist and ValueAddVC: large tech IPOs have mixed first-year results. Now apply that to the $1.8T SpaceX listing, the OpenAI float, and the Anthropic IPO. Exceptional companies do not always make exceptional investments at peak-excitement prices. The best entry is often 12 months later.
items.tweetB: Prediction markets price SpaceX above $1.8T on day one. History says mega-IPOs at peak excitement with perfection priced in tend to underperform. Morningstar agrees. The question is not whether SpaceX is a great company. It is whether $1.8T on Day 1 is a great price.

charts.desc: Chart of large tech IPO first-year return data from Truist/ValueAddVC. Charts inside — worth opening.
